content.verdict: The Four Seasons Resort Marrakech does something very clever: it gives honeymooners the Four Seasons service standard — the polish, the predictability, the exceptional food — inside a 40-acre citrus garden that genuinely feels like Morocco. The location in the Hivernage district is the single strongest argument: you are a 10-minute walk from the Koutoubia and the Medina gate, which no Palmeraie resort can offer. Add an adults-only pool, a 2,500m² spa with a proper Moroccan hammam, and private garden villas for the big-spender honeymoon, and this becomes a top-tier choice for couples who want romance with the option of being in the souks on foot before the mint tea cools.
content.best_room: Book a Premier One-Bedroom Suite with garden terrace and plunge pool, or step up to a two-bedroom Royal Villa with private pool and walled garden if budget allows. The suites are large, light, and give you a private outdoor space away from the main pool and restaurants — ideal for slow honeymoon breakfasts in a robe.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival in Hivernage
itinerary_7_nights.description: Private airport transfer (15 minutes), welcome mint tea and honey pastries on your garden terrace, an early dip in the adults-only pool, dinner at Quattro for a Milanese-Moroccan tasting menu.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Medina on foot
itinerary_7_nights.description: Walk to the Koutoubia at opening time, private guided tour of the souks, Bahia Palace and Saadian Tombs, lunch at Nomad rooftop, afternoon couples hammam and signature massage at the spa.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Slow day by the adults-only pool
itinerary_7_nights.description: Yoga at sunrise in the citrus garden, late breakfast, full day by the Azzera adults-only pool with cabana service, poolside mocktail tasting in the afternoon, casual dinner at Inara Moroccan.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Atlas Mountains escape
itinerary_7_nights.description: Private 4x4 to the Ourika Valley, hike to the seven cascades, Berber lunch riverside, stop at an argan cooperative, return to the resort for a private garden dinner with lanterns and rose petals.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Palace and design day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning at the Dar El Bacha Museum, lunch at Le Jardin, afternoon at Jardin Majorelle and the YSL Museum, tea at La Mamounia's garden, dinner back at the resort in your suite.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Cooking class and date-night dinner
itinerary_7_nights.description: Moroccan cooking class with the executive chef, learn to make tanjia, preserved lemon chicken and m'hanncha, eat your results at the chef's table, pool spa access in the afternoon, rooftop drinks at Bo & Zin elsewhere in Hivernage.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Late morning and departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Breakfast in the garden, final hammam cool-down, late check-out at 16:00 arranged in advance, farewell tea, private transfer to Marrakech Menara airport.

caveats: The Four Seasons formula is polished and international — if you want something unmistakably small, singular and Moroccan (a Medina riad, for example), this will feel too big and too branded.
Hivernage is convenient and safe but not picturesque; the immediate streets outside the gates are modern hotel strip rather than old city atmosphere.
The main pool welcomes families and can be lively; honeymooners should default to the Azzera adults-only pool, which is quieter but smaller and fills up on peak days.

faqs.question: Does Four Seasons Marrakech have an adults-only pool?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Azzera pool is reserved for guests aged 16 and over and offers cabanas, full food and drink service and a much quieter atmosphere than the main family pool.

faqs.question: Can we walk to the Medina?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Koutoubia Mosque is a 10–12 minute walk and the main Jemaa el-Fna square is 15 minutes on foot through Hivernage, which is one of the resort's biggest advantages over Palmeraie addresses.

faqs.question: Is it family-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, there is a kids' club and the main pool welcomes children, but the 40-acre grounds are large enough that honeymooners can find quiet corners, and the adults-only pool provides a kid-free alternative.

faqs.question: Is the spa worth booking in advance?
faqs.answer: Yes. The 2,500m² spa has a traditional hammam, couples treatment suites and a range of Moroccan-inspired rituals using rose, argan and ghassoul; high season treatment slots fill up days ahead.

faqs.question: Do they arrange special honeymoon touches?
faqs.answer: Yes. Mention the occasion at booking and they typically provide an in-room welcome, rose petal turndown, and will set up private dinners in the garden or on your terrace on request.

faqs.question: When is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: March to May and late September to early November give you warm days, cool evenings and the most pleasant Medina walking conditions. Avoid July and August unless you enjoy 40°C heat and shade-seeking.

Subject: Honeymoon booking inquiry — Four Seasons Resort Marrakech

Dear Four Seasons Marrakech reservations team,

We are planning our honeymoon and would like to request availability and your best honeymoon rate for 7 nights from [ARRIVAL DATE] to [DEPARTURE DATE] in a Premier One-Bedroom Suite with garden terrace, and a quote for a Royal Villa with private pool as an upgrade option.

We would also appreciate:
- Private airport transfer from Marrakech Menara (RAK)
- Daily breakfast and any current honeymoon amenities
- A couples hammam ritual at the spa and a private garden dinner during our stay
- Access to the Azzera adults-only pool and cabana reservations
- Late check-out on our departure day

Could you also confirm your deposit and cancellation policy? We are celebrating our marriage and any small gesture to mark the occasion would be very welcome.

Thank you very much, and we look forward to hearing from you.
